title: Color Correction and Shot Matching

video.note: Introduces the Color page, color nodes as correction containers, color management, input color space awareness, primaries, lift, gamma, gain, offset, contrast, saturation, temperature, tint, reset habits, and before/after comparison evidence.

learningTarget: I can make a basic color correction pass that improves exposure, contrast, white balance, and consistency between shots.

bellRinger.prompt: What makes two shots feel like they belong in the same scene?

vocabulary.definition: The Resolve page used for color correction and grading.
Settings that help convert footage from the camera color space into a useful editing and viewing space.
The camera or recording color profile assigned to footage so Resolve interprets it correctly.
A container for color corrections that can be enabled, disabled, reset, or organized in order.
A primary color wheel control that mostly affects darker parts of the image.
A primary color wheel control that mostly affects midtones.
A primary color wheel control that mostly affects brighter parts of the image.
The strength or intensity of color in the image.
Adjusting color temperature and tint so neutral areas look natural.

slides.title: Lesson 08 - Color Correction and Shot Matching

assignment.title: Color Correction Comparison
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a before screenshot.
Google Drive link to an after screenshot from the same shot or sequence.
Short reflection naming at least three color controls tested.

exitTicket: Which color control changed your image most today, and what visual evidence proves it?
